title_en: The Truth About Your Non-Stick Pan
title_es: La Verdad Sobre Tu Sartén Antiadherente
key_message: Switch to healthier cookware like cast iron to avoid harmful PFAS in your food.
visual_elements: Side-by-side with shiny cast iron pan
Text overlay showing 'PFAS' with a red X
Pet bird in the background flying away
Cooking the same egg in both pans with different results
_source_food_context:
DAILY STAPLE COMPARISON [Kitchen Essentials]:
• HEALTHY: Stainless steel, cast iron, and ceramic-coated cookware
• PROCESSED/CONVENTIONAL: Teflon (PTFE) non-stick pans and coated appliances (release toxic fumes above 500°F, coating degrades into PFAS)
• KEY FACT: Non-stick coatings contain PFAS (forever chemicals) that accumulate in the body. Overheated Teflon releases toxic fumes that can kill pet birds and cause polymer fume fever in humans. Cast iron actually adds beneficial iron to your food
• VISUAL IDEA: Show scratched non-stick pan — 'see those scratches? That coating is now in your food and your body. It never breaks down — that's why they're called forever chemicals'

title_en: Cookware Showdown: Teflon vs. Safe Alternatives
title_es: Confrontación de Utensilios: Teflón vs. Alternativas Seguras
hook_en: Can you spot the healthy cookware?
key_message: Choosing stainless steel or ceramic can protect you from toxic chemicals.
visual_elements: Lineup of different cookware: Teflon, stainless steel, ceramic
Text bubbles highlighting 'toxic fumes' vs. 'safe cooking'
Cooking vegetables in each pan, with dramatic differences pointed out
